Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

GRACE OF LORD KRISHNA

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2008-04-19 · 7 answers
Spiritual Masters say
"Apparent wickedness of any person is only a fortuity,an outer mask and hence it can be gotten rid of.Essentially everyone is divine because every being is connected to Lord Krishna who is ultimate truth of this sansar."

If this true,why we are finding itdifficult to transform the bad peple(like decoits,pickpocket,prostitues)
into disciple of Lord Krishna.After all everyone is Krishna Consciousness entity and they are only forgotten devotee.Why the grace of Lord Krishna do not fall upon them
user [2] · 2008-04-20
Krishnas unlimited Grace can be counteracted by the power of freedom to choose given to us by the Lord.
In other words, the Lord does not interfere with our will to stay apart from Him.
He is always waiting, giving us opportunities for the change and ready to accept us back to home, but will never force us to do so.
user [280] · 2008-04-20
If we want to reform so called bad people,it needs tremendous spiritual power.How the power will come to me?
Will Lord help us to attain these objective?
user [2] · 2008-04-20
only one word: compassion, and that only comes with practical knowledge of the suffering in the material world and the experience of spiritual joy side by side.
user [280] · 2008-04-21
Suppose,I want to reform a notorious decoit.It is obvoious that he will be in no mood to listen spiritual words.What is the practicle approach to deal with this problem? How can I take him away from bad association? I have observed that he is enjoying the company of bad people rather than good people.
user [2] · 2008-04-21
if the person is not agreeable and negatively inclined even to chant, then our secret weapon is prasadam, let the person take regularly some form of prasadam and you will see miracles happening even in this very life. But all this must be accompanied by true compassion.
user [38] · 2008-04-22
And praying on his behalf.
user [149] · 2008-04-22
and you can always ask Krsna to give him the benefit of your pious and devotional activities.
